title: [VPlan] Supported conditionally executed single early exits.
author: fhahn
description:
Add support for a single early exit that is executed conditionally. To make sure the mask from any non-exiting control flow is combined with the early exit condition.

To do so, introduce a MaskedCond VPInstruction, which is inserted as user of the early-exit condition, at the point of the early-exit branch. The VPInstruction will get masked automatically if needed by the predicator, ensuring that we properly account for it when checking whether the early exit has been taken.

Note that this does not allow for instructions that require predication after the early exit. This requires additional work in progress: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/172454

As an alternative to MaskedCond, we could also predicate before handling early exiting blocks: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/181830

tests.test_body:
declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @uncountable_exit_on_last_block() {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %search, %entry
  %index = phi i64 [ %index.next, %search ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %index.next = add i64 %index, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %index.next, 67
  br i1 %exitcond, label %search, label %loop.end

search:                                           ; preds = %loop
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %index
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %arrayidx1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %index
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx1, align 1
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld2
  br i1 %cmp3, label %loop.end, label %loop

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %search, %loop
  %retval = phi i64 [ 64, %loop ], [ %index, %search ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @same_exit_block_pre_inc_use1_with_reduction() {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.inc, %entry
  %index = phi i64 [ %index.next, %loop.inc ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %red = phi i64 [ %red.next, %loop.inc ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %index
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %arrayidx1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %index
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx1, align 1
  %ld2.zext = zext i8 %ld2 to i64
  %red.next = add i64 %red, %ld2.zext
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld2
  br i1 %cmp3, label %loop.inc, label %loop.end

loop.inc:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %index.next = add i64 %index, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %index.next, 67
  br i1 %exitcond, label %loop, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.inc, %loop
  %final.ind = phi i64 [ %index, %loop ], [ 67, %loop.inc ]
  %retval = add i64 %red.next, %final.ind
  ret i64 %retval
}

define void @loop_contains_store_uncounted_exit_is_not_guaranteed_to_execute(ptr noalias dereferenceable(40) %array, ptr readonly align 2 dereferenceable(40) %pred) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.inc, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %for.inc ]
  %st.addr = getelementptr inbounds nuw i16, ptr %array, i64 %iv
  %data = load i16, ptr %st.addr, align 2
  %inc = add nsw i16 %data, 1
  store i16 %inc, ptr %st.addr, align 2
  %rem = urem i64 %iv, 5
  %skip.ee.cmp = icmp eq i64 %rem, 0
  br i1 %skip.ee.cmp, label %for.inc, label %ee.block

ee.block:                                         ; preds = %for.body
  %ee.addr = getelementptr inbounds nuw i16, ptr %pred, i64 %iv
  %ee.val = load i16, ptr %ee.addr, align 2
  %ee.cond = icmp sgt i16 %ee.val, 500
  br i1 %ee.cond, label %exit, label %for.inc

for.inc:                                          ; preds = %ee.block, %for.body
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %counted.cond = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 20
  br i1 %counted.cond, label %exit, label %for.body

exit:                                             ; preds = %for.inc, %ee.block
  ret void
}

@A = external global [64 x i8]

define i64 @exit_cond_defined_in_header() {
entry:
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr @A, i64 %iv
  %l.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %branch.cond = icmp slt i8 %l.A, 0
  %exit.cond = icmp eq i8 %l.A, 1
  br i1 %branch.cond, label %block.a, label %loop.latch

block.a:                                          ; preds = %loop.header
  br i1 %exit.cond, label %loop.end, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %block.a, %loop.header
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 64
  br i1 %ec, label %loop.header, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.latch, %block.a
  %retval = phi i64 [ 10, %block.a ], [ %iv, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

@A = external global [64 x i8]
@B = external global [64 x i8]

define i64 @single_exit_in_conditional_block2() {
entry:
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr @A, i64 %iv
  %l.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %branch.cond = icmp slt i8 %l.A, 0
  br i1 %branch.cond, label %block.a, label %merge

block.a:                                          ; preds = %loop.header
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr @B, i64 %iv
  %l.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp = icmp eq i8 %l.A, %l.B
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop.end, label %merge

merge:                                            ; preds = %block.a, %loop.header
  br label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %merge
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 64
  br i1 %ec, label %loop.header, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.latch, %block.a
  %retval = phi i64 [ %iv, %block.a ], [ -1, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

@A = external global [64 x i8]
@B = external global [64 x i8]

define i64 @single_exit_in_conditional_block() {
entry:
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr @A, i64 %iv
  %l.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %branch.cond = icmp slt i8 %l.A, 0
  br i1 %branch.cond, label %block.a, label %loop.latch

block.a:                                          ; preds = %loop.header
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr @B, i64 %iv
  %l.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %cmp = icmp eq i8 %l.A, %l.B
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop.end, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %block.a, %loop.header
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 64
  br i1 %ec, label %loop.header, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.latch, %block.a
  %retval = phi i64 [ %iv, %block.a ], [ -1, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

@A = external global [64 x i8]

define i64 @livein_exit_cond_in_conditional(i1 %exit.cond) {
entry:
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr @A, i64 %iv
  %l.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %branch.cond = icmp slt i8 %l.A, 0
  br i1 %branch.cond, label %block.a, label %loop.latch

block.a:                                          ; preds = %loop.header
  br i1 %exit.cond, label %loop.end, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %block.a, %loop.header
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 64
  br i1 %ec, label %loop.header, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.latch, %block.a
  %retval = phi i64 [ %iv, %block.a ], [ 99, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

@A = external global [64 x i8]
@B = external global [64 x i8]
@C = external global [64 x i8]

define i64 @diamond_with_join_then_exit() {
entry:
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr @A, i64 %iv
  %l.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %branch.cond = icmp slt i8 %l.A, 0
  br i1 %branch.cond, label %block.a, label %block.b

block.a:                                          ; preds = %loop.header
  %gep.B = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr @B, i64 %iv
  %l.B = load i8, ptr %gep.B, align 1
  %val.a = zext i8 %l.B to i64
  br label %join

block.b:                                          ; preds = %loop.header
  %gep.C = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr @C, i64 %iv
  %l.C = load i8, ptr %gep.C, align 1
  %val.b = zext i8 %l.C to i64
  br label %join

join:                                             ; preds = %block.b, %block.a
  %val = phi i64 [ %val.a, %block.a ], [ %val.b, %block.b ]
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %val, 42
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop.end, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %join
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 64
  br i1 %ec, label %loop.header, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.latch, %join
  %retval = phi i64 [ %val, %join ], [ 0, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

@A = external global [64 x i8]

define i64 @livein_exit_cond_in_conditional2(i1 %exit.cond) {
entry:
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %gep.A = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr @A, i64 %iv
  %l.A = load i8, ptr %gep.A, align 1
  %branch.cond = icmp slt i8 %l.A, 0
  br i1 %branch.cond, label %block.a, label %loop.latch

block.a:                                          ; preds = %loop.header
  br i1 %exit.cond, label %loop.end, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %block.a, %loop.header
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp ne i64 %iv.next, 64
  br i1 %ec, label %loop.header, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.latch, %block.a
  %retval = phi i64 [ 99, %block.a ], [ %iv, %loop.latch ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

declare void @init_mem(ptr, i64)

define i64 @same_exit_block_pre_inc1_use_inv_cond(i1 %cond) {
entry:
  %p1 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  %p2 = alloca [1024 x i8], align 1
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p1, i64 1024)
  call void @init_mem(ptr %p2, i64 1024)
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.inc, %entry
  %index = phi i64 [ %index.next, %loop.inc ], [ 3, %entry ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p1, i64 %index
  %ld1 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx, align 1
  %arrayidx1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %p2, i64 %index
  %ld2 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx1, align 1
  %cmp3 = icmp eq i8 %ld1, %ld2
  %cmp4 = select i1 %cond, i1 %cmp3, i1 false
  br i1 %cmp4, label %loop.inc, label %loop.end

loop.inc:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %index.next = add i64 %index, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %index.next, 67
  br i1 %exitcond, label %loop, label %loop.end

loop.end:                                         ; preds = %loop.inc, %loop
  %retval = phi i64 [ %index, %loop ], [ 67, %loop.inc ]
  ret i64 %retval
}

comments.author: llvm-ci
comments.body:
LLVM Buildbot has detected a new failure on builder `sanitizer-aarch64-linux` running on `sanitizer-buildbot7` while building `llvm` at step 2 "annotate".

Full details are available at: https://lab.llvm.org/buildbot/#/builders/51/builds/32637

comments.author: llvm-ci
comments.body:
LLVM Buildbot has detected a new failure on builder `sanitizer-aarch64-linux-bootstrap-msan` running on `sanitizer-buildbot10` while building `llvm` at step 2 "annotate".

Full details are available at: https://lab.llvm.org/buildbot/#/builders/94/builds/15653

<details>
<summary>Here is the relevant piece of the build log for the reference</summary>
